title: Extract shared batch-spy fixture in TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch
analysis: The TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch class (line 941) has two test methods — test_merge_uses_batch_fetch (line 944) and test_merge_equal_size_clusters_first_in_request_wins (line 985) — that each contain ~10 identical lines: (1) a _n1_trap function that raises AssertionError if get_activity_cluster is called, (2) monkeypatch.setattr for the N+1 trap, (3) a batch_args list, (4) capture of _original_batch, (5) a _batch_spy closure that records calls, and (6) monkeypatch.setattr for the batch spy. The only differences between the tests are the seed data and the final assertions. Extracting into a private _install_batch_spy method that returns batch_args eliminates the duplication while keeping each test's scenario-specific setup and assertions in place. This is a pure refactoring — no behavior changes, so existing tests serve as the regression safety net.

prerequisites.description: Verify existing tests in TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch pass before refactoring: run pytest tests/test_cluster_review.py::TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch -v

steps.description: Add a focused unit test test_install_batch_spy_returns_list_and_traps_n1 to TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch that calls self._install_batch_spy(monkeypatch) and asserts: (a) it returns an empty list, (b) calling CatalogDB.get_activity_cluster raises AssertionError containing 'N+1 detected', (c) after calling get_activity_clusters_by_ids via the app's DB, the returned list has one entry recording the call args. This test will fail because _install_batch_spy does not exist yet.

Implement _install_batch_spy(self, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> list[list[str]] on TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch. The method defines _n1_trap (raises AssertionError on single-cluster fetch), patches CatalogDB.get_activity_cluster, creates batch_args list, captures _original_batch = CatalogDB.get_activity_clusters_by_ids, defines _batch_spy closure that appends to batch_args and delegates to original, patches CatalogDB.get_activity_clusters_by_ids, and returns batch_args. Verify step-1 test passes.

Refactor test_merge_uses_batch_fetch to replace the inline _n1_trap/_batch_spy setup (lines 955-968) with a single call: batch_args = self._install_batch_spy(monkeypatch). Keep seed data (_seed_cluster_via_db calls) and all assertions unchanged. Run to confirm it passes — this validates the helper produces identical behavior to the inline code.

Refactor test_merge_equal_size_clusters_first_in_request_wins to replace the inline _n1_trap/_batch_spy setup (lines 1002-1015) with a single call: batch_args = self._install_batch_spy(monkeypatch). Keep seed data and assertions unchanged. Run full TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch suite (all 3 tests) to confirm all pass.
Run the full test_cluster_review.py test suite to confirm no regressions from the refactoring. All existing tests must pass unchanged.

design_decisions.decision: Use a private method _install_batch_spy on the class rather than a module-level pytest fixture
design_decisions.rationale: The batch spy is tightly coupled to the TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch class and its monkeypatch of CatalogDB. A class method keeps scope narrow, avoids polluting the module-level fixture namespace, and matches the task description's suggested approach.

design_decisions.decision: Return batch_args (the mutable list) from the helper rather than a more complex object
design_decisions.rationale: Both tests only need the batch_args list to make assertions. Returning the bare list keeps the API minimal. Since Python lists are mutable, the spy closure will continue appending to the returned list as calls happen.

design_decisions.decision: Add a dedicated test for _install_batch_spy itself before refactoring existing tests
design_decisions.rationale: While existing tests serve as regression safety, a focused test for the helper validates it installs both the N+1 trap and the batch spy correctly in isolation. This gives confidence before swapping out the inline code in the two existing tests.

design_decisions.decision: Steps 3 and 4 are split (refactor one test at a time) rather than changing both at once
design_decisions.rationale: Atomic changes — if a refactored test fails, we know exactly which test's conversion caused the issue. Each step can be independently verified and committed.

reuse.what: Existing _n1_trap pattern (raise AssertionError on single-cluster fetch)
reuse.how: Move verbatim into the _install_batch_spy helper — no changes to the trap logic

reuse.what: Existing _batch_spy closure pattern (capture original, wrap with recording)
reuse.how: Move verbatim into the _install_batch_spy helper — the closure, original capture, and monkeypatch calls are identical
Tests continue using this to set up scenario-specific seed data before calling _install_batch_spy
